scope: Final independent verification against the MVP acceptance criteria and quality gates in reports/autocomplete-engine-mvp-plan-2026-05-28.json.

overall_assessment.summary: The broker/protocol/provider path is working and all plan quality gates verified here pass, including a fresh mock broker smoke and a fresh real pi-backed synthetic TypeScript and Markdown smoke. Final acceptance is not fully achieved because real VS Code and Obsidian GUI ghost-text/Tab behavior remains unproven, and an added strict static check (`cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`) currently fails on style lints.
overall_assessment.mvp_working_statement: Working for broker-level mock and pi synthetic autocomplete over the shared local HTTP protocol; not yet independently accepted as an end-to-end editor GUI MVP.

commands_run.result: completed before current-task writes; showed pre-existing unrelated deleted legacy reports/orchestration/* entries plus untracked orchestration/archive/worker paths. These were not modified for this verification.

passed. Rust workspace tests all passed: 39 tests across autocomplete-addons, autocomplete-core, autocomplete-protocol, autocomplete-provider-pi, autocomplete-server, plus doc tests with zero failures.
passed. Dev workspace build finished successfully.

failed. Clippy reported default-constructed unit struct lints in crates/autocomplete-addons/src/lib.rs, crates/autocomplete-provider-pi/src/parser.rs, and crates/autocomplete-provider-pi/src/provider.rs, plus clone-on-copy in crates/autocomplete-provider-pi/src/provider.rs. This is a static-check blocker, not a runtime acceptance failure.
passed. 9 Node tests passed across shared protocol, VS Code host glue, Obsidian host glue, cancellation, and provider-boundary checks.

commands_run.command: mock broker external HTTP smoke on 127.0.0.1:32146 using a temporary TOML config
commands_run.result: passed. /health returned protocol_version=1, status=ok, provider mock/available. POST /v1/autocomplete returned status=ok with source=mock and postprocessed=true for one vscode/typescript request and one obsidian/markdown request. Wall-clock latencies were 0.847 ms and 0.597 ms.

commands_run.command: timeout 20s pi --list-models | grep for gpt-5.5 provider availability
commands_run.result: completed. Output on this host included openai-codex gpt-5.5; openai/gpt-5.5 was not observed in the filtered output.

commands_run.command: pi broker external HTTP smoke on 127.0.0.1:32147 using provider=openai-codex, model=gpt-5.5, thinking=minimal, timeout_ms=10000
commands_run.result: passed. /health returned provider name pi:gpt-5.5 and status unknown. A synthetic vscode/typescript request returned status=ok, insert_text=" n + 1", confidence=0.99, source=pi:gpt-5.5, postprocessed=true, wall_ms=4673.216. A synthetic obsidian/markdown request returned status=ok, insert_text=" the core latency and throughput numbers", confidence=0.72, source=pi:gpt-5.5, postprocessed=true, wall_ms=5098.615.
passed. No provider-specific implementation imports or model/provider selections were found in clients/. The npm boundary test also passed.

mvp_acceptance_criteria.criterion: A single broker serves both VS Code and Obsidian.
evidence: crates/autocomplete-server exposes one /v1/autocomplete endpoint through app(...).
Fresh mock smoke served both a vscode/typescript request and an obsidian/markdown request through the same broker process.
Fresh pi smoke served both a vscode/typescript request and an obsidian/markdown request through the same broker process.

mvp_acceptance_criteria.criterion: VS Code and Obsidian clients call the same protocol.
evidence: clients/vscode/src/extension-core.js and clients/obsidian/src/plugin-core.js both import ../../protocol/src/client.js.
clients/protocol/src/generated/protocol-v1.js defines /v1/autocomplete and /v1/cancel/ shared constants.
npm test --prefix clients passed shared protocol, VS Code, and Obsidian request-construction tests.

mvp_acceptance_criteria.criterion: Switching provider from mock to pi requires no client changes.
evidence: BrokerConfig selects provider.kind = mock or pi in crates/autocomplete-server/src/lib.rs; clients expose broker URL/debounce/context/deadline settings only.
The fresh mock and pi smokes used the same HTTP endpoint and client-shaped request payloads; only broker TOML config changed.
No provider-specific code or model/provider setting was found under clients/.

mvp_acceptance_criteria.criterion: VS Code displays inline ghost text and accepts it with Tab.
evidence: Source path exists: clients/vscode/src/extension.js registers an InlineCompletionItemProvider and clients/vscode/src/extension-core.js returns vscode.InlineCompletionItem for broker insert_text.
Node host-glue test passed: VS Code inline provider cancels stale requests before rendering ghost text.

Limitation: no real VS Code Extension Development Host smoke was run in this verification or documented previous smoke; Tab acceptance depends on VS Code inline-completion keybinding behavior and remains GUI-unproven.

mvp_acceptance_criteria.criterion: Obsidian displays inline ghost text and accepts it with Tab.
evidence: Source path exists: clients/obsidian/src/main.js defines GhostTextWidget, CodeMirror Decoration.widget rendering, and a high-priority Tab keymap that calls accept(view).
Node host-glue test passed: Obsidian controller cancels stale note requests and accepts fresh ghost text with Tab by dispatching insertion.
Limitation: no disposable-vault Obsidian GUI smoke was run; runtime module resolution and actual editor keymap interaction remain unproven.
clients/protocol/src/client.js implements BrokerAutocompleteSession with debounce timers, AbortController cancellation, and broker /v1/cancel calls for stale requests.
crates/autocomplete-server tracks inflight request CancellationToken values and implements POST /v1/cancel/{request_id}.
cargo test passed engine timeout/cancellation and server cancel endpoint tests; npm test passed client stale-cancellation tests for shared protocol, VS Code, and Obsidian.

mvp_acceptance_criteria.criterion: pi provider can complete simple code and Markdown examples.
evidence: Fresh pi smoke with provider=openai-codex and model=gpt-5.5 returned a plausible TypeScript insert_text " n + 1" for an increment function within 4673.216 ms.
Fresh pi smoke returned a Markdown insert_text " the core latency and throughput numbers" within 5098.615 ms.
Notes: evidence is synthetic and latency is too slow for per-keystroke autocomplete; docs correctly describe manual/idle MVP suitability only.

mvp_acceptance_criteria.criterion: Core tests cover protocol validation, provider parsing, postprocessing, timeout, and cancellation.
evidence: Protocol validation tests passed in crates/autocomplete-protocol/tests/validation.rs and fixture/schema tests.
Provider parsing and repair tests passed in crates/autocomplete-provider-pi/tests/pi_provider.rs and parser unit tests.
Postprocessing tests passed in crates/autocomplete-core/tests/postprocessors.rs.
Timeout and cancellation tests passed in crates/autocomplete-core/tests/engine.rs, crates/autocomplete-provider-pi/tests/pi_provider.rs, and crates/autocomplete-server/tests/http_endpoints.rs.

quality_gates.gate: No platform client imports provider-specific code.
evidence: npm provider-boundary test passed.
Manual client search found no provider-pi/PiProvider/openai/gpt/pi-specific implementation imports or provider selections under clients/.

quality_gates.gate: No autocomplete request enables pi tools.
evidence: crates/autocomplete-provider-pi/src/provider.rs command_args include --no-tools, --no-session, --no-context-files, --no-extensions, --no-skills, --no-prompt-templates, and --no-themes.
pi_provider_invokes_pi_with_isolation_flags_and_parses_jsonl_success asserts required isolation flags.

quality_gates.gate: No autocomplete request implicitly reads arbitrary files.
evidence: pi provider command_args include --no-context-files and --no-session.

MVP addons only transform request-supplied prefix/suffix and do not read workspace files; ContextConfig include_open_files/include_workspace_symbols default to false and have no file-indexing implementation.

quality_gates.gate: All provider outputs pass postprocessing before reaching clients.
evidence: AutocompleteEngine calls self.postprocessors.process before constructing AutocompleteResponse::Ok.
Fresh mock and pi smokes returned metadata.postprocessed=true for ok responses.
Postprocessor tests cover markdown fences, newline normalization, repeated prefix handling through engine test, max chars, and explanation rejection.

quality_gates.gate: All requests have deadline and cancellation handling.
evidence: AutocompleteOptions requires deadline_ms and validation enforces 1..=30000 ms by default.
AutocompleteEngine wraps provider future in tokio::time::timeout and cancellation token select.
Server /v1/cancel and client stale-cancellation tests passed.

quality_gates.gate: Sensitive path exclusions are applied before remote provider calls.
evidence: crates/autocomplete-server/src/lib.rs calls snapshot.addons.prepare(request) before engine.complete_with_provider_context.
PolicyEngine rejects excluded paths and oversized remote context before provider execution.
privacy_policy_rejects_excluded_paths_before_provider_runs and privacy_policy_rejects_context_over_remote_byte_limit tests passed.
README.md and docs/client-setup.md describe broker startup, mock config, pi config with provider=openai-codex/model=gpt-5.5, and provider switching without client changes.

README.md and docs/limitations-and-next-steps.md explicitly state pi latency limitations, synthetic-only pi quality evidence, default openai/gpt-5.5 API-key failure on this host, missing real VS Code/Obsidian GUI smoke, packaging caveats, and provider-source diagnostic limitation.
The prior MVP-050 smoke handoff's latency figures and limitations are reflected accurately in the docs; the fresh pi smoke is directionally consistent with those limitations.

blocker_fix_tasks.title: Run real editor-host acceptance smoke
blocker_fix_tasks.details: Use VS Code Extension Development Host and a disposable Obsidian vault. Verify visible ghost text and Tab acceptance with mock provider first, then one bounded pi request per editor if practical. Record exact setup, results, and any keybinding/module-resolution defects.
blocks: VS Code displays inline ghost text and accepts it with Tab
Obsidian displays inline ghost text and accepts it with Tab

blocker_fix_tasks.title: Make strict clippy pass
blocker_fix_tasks.details: Fix clippy -D warnings by replacing default() calls for unit structs with direct unit struct construction in autocomplete-addons/provider-pi and removing the clone-on-copy SecretRedactor clone. Then rerun cargo fmt --all --check, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, and cargo test.

recommended_follow_ups.title: Package/bundle clients after GUI smoke
recommended_follow_ups.details: Current docs correctly call clients local-source MVPs relying on repository-relative clients/protocol imports. Packaging is not required by the MVP plan but should be solved before broader use.

recommended_follow_ups.title: Improve pi diagnostics
recommended_follow_ups.details: Health/source currently report pi:gpt-5.5 and omit configured provider=openai-codex. This is documented and not a final-acceptance blocker, but it would make future smoke reports clearer.

safety_notes: No implementation or documentation corrections were made in this verification; only this handoff report was written.
Did not read prompt-buffer.md, repo tmp/, archive contents, or unrelated directories under ~/dev.
Real pi calls used synthetic TypeScript and Markdown contexts only.
Pre-existing unrelated dirty/untracked worktree entries were preserved.
